auth_config_env: R2R_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATION (not set in docker/env/r2r-full.env; toml default require_authentication=false governs)
R2R_CONFIG_NAME=full (selects config that inherits the require_authentication=false default)
R2R_SECRET_KEY (JWT signing key; blank in template)

default_creds.context: Default superuser created at init by R2RAuthProvider from r2r.toml [auth] default_admin_email/default_admin_password. With require_authentication=false (default) anonymous callers are auto-mapped to this user; if auth is later armed without rotating, these creds log in as superuser.

install_tell: git clone https://github.com/SciPhi-AI/R2R && cd R2R/docker && export R2R_CONFIG_NAME=full OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... && docker compose -f compose.full.yaml --profile postgres up -d   # API at http://localhost:7272 , dashboard at :7273
misconfig_patterns: Quickstart `docker compose ... up -d` binds 7272 and the env template never sets R2R_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATION, so the default require_authentication=false leaves every /v3 endpoint anonymous-superuser the moment the port is internet-reachable
Operators exposing 7272 without a fronting reverse-proxy / auth gateway (R2R has no built-in TLS or network ACL by default)
Default superuser password change_me_immediately left unrotated even after arming auth
Provider API keys placed in r2r-full.env become readable via /v3/system/settings the instant the box is reachable, because the superuser-only gate is satisfied by anonymous auto-elevation

response_markers: response wrapped in a top-level "results" envelope (R2R-unique)
GET /v3/system/settings returns 200 with a config TOML dump (config_toml) and r2r_project_name when auth is default-off (superuser-only check satisfied by anonymous auto-elevation)
active_probe.false_positive_check: "v3/health" and "results"/"message":"ok" are individually generic and collide with countless APIs. Disambiguate by requiring the R2R-specific COMBINATION on port 7272: (1) GET /v3/health -> 200 with exactly {"results":{"message":"ok"}} (the "results" envelope is R2R's response wrapper, not a bare {"status":"ok"}), AND (2) GET /v3/system/settings -> 200 returning a JSON object containing "config" + "prompts" + "r2r_project_name" keys (config dumped from to_toml). A host that returns the health 200 but 401/403 on /v3/system/settings is either a hardened R2R (auth armed) or a lookalike; a host returning config_toml + r2r_project_name is positively R2R with default-off auth. Do not fingerprint on bare "RAG"/"retrieval"/"chat" strings. Port 7273 (Next.js dashboard) alone is a weak tell since it is a generic Next.js app on :3000 internally; anchor on the 7272 /v3 envelope instead.

deployment_tells: Three adjacent published ports 7272 (API) / 7273 (R2R Next.js dashboard) / 7274 (Hatchet orchestration dashboard) on the same host is a strong R2R-full signature
Container/compose project name r2r_default (R2R_PROJECT_NAME default) and bundled services: postgres+pgvector, hatchet, unstructured, clustering
Server header / app framework: FastAPI (uvicorn) serving /v3 under a top-level "results" response envelope
Dashboard is a Next.js app (R2R-Application) typically reachable on 7273 mapping to internal :3000
pivot_paths: GET /v3/system/settings -> harvest upstream LLM/provider API keys from the config_toml dump (read-only)
GET /v3/documents then GET /v3/documents/{id}/chunks -> enumerate and read the ingested private corpus (names/metadata first, restraint-ethic: enumerate not exfiltrate)
GET /v3/users -> confirm the claimable default admin and roster
GET /v3/prompts and the settings prompt dump -> read system prompts / business logic
POST /v3/retrieval/search (read-only query) -> confirm RAG surface is anonymously queryable and corpus is live, without ingesting or mutating
vulnerabilities: No platform-specific CVE/GHSA published for SciPhi-AI/R2R as of 2026-06-12 (GitHub Security tab and NVD return no R2R-specific advisory). The exposure is a default-misconfiguration class (insecure default), not a numbered CVE.

Hardcoded default superuser credential admin@example.com / change_me_immediately shipped in r2r.toml; documentation provides no in-band rotation path (tracked in upstream issues #1234, #1570) - a credential-stuffing / claimable-admin condition on any deployment that flipped auth ON without changing the default password.
auth_evidence: Default ships auth OFF. Primary source `py/r2r/r2r.toml` [auth] section sets verbatim: provider = "r2r", require_authentication = false, require_email_verification = false, default_admin_email = "admin@example.com", default_admin_password = "change_me_immediately". The quickstart compose loads `docker/env/r2r-full.env`, which does NOT set R2R_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATION (auth absent from the entire env file), so the toml default governs unchanged. The runtime auth_wrapper in py/core/base/providers/auth.py auto-elevates anonymous callers to the default admin: `if (((not self.config.require_authentication) or public) and auth is None and api_key is None): return await self._get_default_admin_user()` where `_get_default_admin_user` resolves the user by `self.admin_email` (admin@example.com). Net result: a documented quickstart `docker compose -f compose.full.yaml --profile postgres up -d` exposes port 7272 where every /v3 REST call is served as the superuser with no credential. This is auth-exists-but-OFF-by-default, not open-by-default (the provider machinery is present and a single env/toml flip arms it).
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM/provider API keys and endpoints exposed via GET /v3/system/settings config_toml dump (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, AZURE/VERTEX/GEMINI/MISTRAL/GROQ/COHERE keys, plus FIRECRAWL/SERPER/TAVILY/UNSTRUCTURED/SENDGRID/MAILERSEND keys all flow through r2r-full.env into config)
Ingested RAG corpus: full document text and metadata listable via GET /v3/documents and retrievable chunk-by-chunk via /v3/chunks (the customer's private knowledge base / PII)
Knowledge-graph entities, relationships and communities via /v3/graphs revealing business-domain structure
System prompts and prompt templates via /v3/prompts and the settings dump (business logic / instruction leakage)
Vector index configuration and search surface via /v3/indices and /v3/retrieval (RAG and hybrid search executable anonymously, burning the operator's LLM credits)
Full user roster and the claimable default superuser (admin@example.com / change_me_immediately) via /v3/users
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
